to: consider the child's welfare as the paramount consideration

conditions.text: determining any question with respect to the upbringing of a child or the administration of a child’s property

to: have regard to the general principle that any delay in determining the question is likely to prejudice the welfare of the child

conditions.text: any proceedings in which any question with respect to the upbringing of a child arises

to: presume that involvement of each parent will further the child's welfare
have regard in particular to the child's wishes, needs, and other factors
make an order unless it considers that doing so would be better for the child than making no order at all

conditions.text: a court is considering whether or not to make one or more orders under this Act with respect to a child

to: have parental responsibility for the child

conditions.text: they were married to, or civil partners of, each other at the time of the child's birth

to: have parental responsibility for the child

conditions.text: the child's father and mother were not married to, or civil partners of, each other at the time of his birth

to: have parental responsibility for the child

conditions.text: he has acquired it in accordance with the provisions of this Act

to: act alone in meeting that responsibility

conditions.text: more than one person has parental responsibility for a child

to: act in any way which would be incompatible with any order made with respect to the child under this Act
surrender or transfer any part of that responsibility to another

for: person who does not have parental responsibility but has care of the child
to: do what is reasonable for the purpose of safeguarding or promoting the child’s welfare
acquire parental responsibility for the child

conditions.text: he becomes registered as the child’s father, makes a parental responsibility agreement, or the court orders it

for: person who has acquired parental responsibility

to: appoint an individual to be the child’s guardian

conditions.text: the child has no parent with parental responsibility, or a parent, guardian, or special guardian named in a child arrangements order has died

to: appoint another individual to be the child’s guardian in the event of his death

for: person appointed as a child’s guardian
to: have parental responsibility for the child

conditions.text: it is done by an instrument in writing signed by him and made within a reasonable time of his first knowing that the appointment has taken effect

for: authority or officer of the Service or a Welsh family proceedings officer
to: comply with any request for a report under this section
